mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters. It simplifies the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration compared to standalone RabbitMQ deployments.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repo
steps.description:
This step adds the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

```

> This command adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the deployment of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.0 --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace

```

> This command installs the operator in a new namespace called 'rabbitmq'.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> This command applies the example RabbitMQ cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> This command lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> This command uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.0 --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> This command drains the RabbitMQ pods before upgrading.
> This command upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> This command verifies that the RabbitMQ pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources allocated to the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes cluster has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM per node.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on RabbitMQ pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RabbitMQ pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to RabbitMQ server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RabbitMQ service is not properly exposed or the configuration is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure that the RabbitMQ service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied' when accessing RabbitMQ
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to RBAC settings not allowing access to the RabbitMQ resources.

**Fix:**
Check and update the RBAC roles and bindings to ensure proper permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved health checks, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods in the 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster with the necessary tools installed.
